purpose: Track primary-source candidates for Level 4 quantitative biological calibration. Entries are leads, not fit-ready data.
rules: Do not fit against a lead unless numeric x/y rows are present in a curve CSV with primary-source provenance.
Secondary summaries can help locate papers but cannot define target values.
Prefer empirical ant biology papers over pure mathematical, robotics or ant-colony-optimization references.

leads.title: Decay rates of attractive and repellent pheromones in an ant foraging trail network
leads.notes: Search found this as a likely primary Insectes Sociaux paper through secondary reference lists. Do not enter decay values until the primary paper, figure or table is available.

leads.title: Individual rules for trail pattern formation in Argentine ants
leads.notes: Useful for pheromone-response geometry and Weber-law style response, not a direct decay-rate curve unless figure data are digitized.

leads.title: Modulation of pheromone trail strength with food quality in Pharaoh's ant, Monomorium pharaonis
leads.notes: High-priority empirical candidate for food-quality to recruitment/trail-laying calibration.

leads.title: Optimal traffic organisation in ants under crowded conditions
leads.notes: Candidate for speed-density or flow-density target curves.

leads.title: Trafficlike collective movement of ants on trails: absence of a jammed phase
leads.notes: Candidate independent holdout for no-jam traffic validation after Dussutour-style fitting.
